posts.name: Jukan
posts.text:
Taiwan Makers Poised to Raise Chip Resistor Prices as China Players Hike and the Price Gap Narrows

As Taiwanese chip resistor makers shift to selective order taking and cull orders that carry no profit, low price chip resistor orders are flowing to Chinese chip resistor makers. Ralec (厚聲), the world's second largest chip resistor maker, and Guangdong Fenghua (風華高科), China's leading passive components player, have successively notified distributors of chip resistor price increases. Because a certain price gap exists between Taiwanese and Chinese chip resistors, the convergence of that gap leaves room for Taiwanese makers to set up the next round of price hikes.

According to the supply chain, ahead of the Dragon Boat Festival holiday Guangdong Fenghua notified distributors of an across the board increase in chip resistor prices, with the magnitude reaching 20% to 30%. Prior to this, Ralec had already notified distributors of price increases for resistors in the 0201 to 1206 size range, with new prices effective from June 21. However, the increase Fenghua announced before the holiday far exceeded the level Ralec had set internally, prompting Ralec to make the dramatic move of withdrawing its quotations before the holiday and demanding that prices be renegotiated, which became a focal point of industry discussion.

Chip resistor makers note that as utilization rates at Taiwanese plants gradually climb, major domestic players have already moved to selective order taking, culling low price orders that carry no profit. These low price orders have flowed to Chinese makers, causing order volumes at Chinese makers to surge. However, Chinese makers had not passed through the earlier cost increases driven by the sharp rise in silver prices, creating a predicament in which the more orders they take, the more they lose. Chinese makers are caught in a dilemma over whether to accept orders, and raising prices has become an unavoidable choice.

The industry's read is that the source of this round of price increases still lies in the sharp rise in silver prices. Compared with the same period a year earlier, the silver price is three times higher, and as this feeds through to silver paste it has dealt a heavy blow to the cost structure of chip resistors.

According to supply chain analysis, Yageo (國巨)* and its affiliate Kamet (凱美) are leading the chip resistor price increases. Yageo's new prices already took effect on February 1, and in January Kamet raised thick film resistor prices, while its subsidiary Viking Tech (旺詮) raised metal plate resistor prices by 20% to 30% from April, driven mainly by demand from AI, industrial control, energy storage and new energy. Among these, Yageo is the world's largest chip resistor maker, with a revenue based market share exceeding 30%. On NVIDIA's bill of materials, only three companies globally are listed: Yageo, Rohm and KOA. Reportedly, resistors that enter NVIDIA's BOM start at twice the price of standard specifications.

Meanwhile, chip resistors currently account for roughly 20% of Walsin Tech's (華新科) revenue, and this year the company has more than doubled its capacity for special specification resistors. In the second half, Walsin will progressively negotiate with direct customers on prices for chip resistors and certain MLCC specifications.

In addition, the supply chain has observed that although resistor makers are currently raising prices only for distributors, prices for direct customers have in fact already risen as well. These direct customers are the largest outlet for passive component makers' capacity, so the price increase effect extends beyond distributors and its contribution to revenue is more pronounced.
posts.summary: Taiwan Makers Poised to Raise Chip Resistor Prices as China Players Hike and the Price Gap Narrows As Taiwanese chip resistor makers shift to selective order taking and cull orders that carry no profit, low price chip r...

posts.name: 宝玉
posts.text:
Skill 和软件一样，需要不断迭代的，而且你用户越多，遇到的各种问题就越多，就需要去解决各种边边角角的问题，才能越来越好用。

比如 @yangyi 昨天在他的牛马AI里面测试了这个Skill，说导出好像有问题，我拿到结果一看，简直惨不忍睹（图2），这里面有两个问题：
1. 样式表不对，没有铺满整页，只占了一半
2. 导出的渐变色没有了，把图片都遮没了

写 Skill 比写软件有个优势，就是你可以让 Agent 先跑，跑完之后 Agent 它自己知道有哪些上下文，遇到了什么问题，这样它可以分析问题在哪。

于是我在本地跑一次，能重现，再让它分析原因，解决，那么它就能找出原因，并从 Skill 的层面去解决，添加测试覆盖，避免类似的问题再次出现。图1就是修复后的，看起来就好多了。

这其实也是我日常迭代 Skill 的方法：

自己用 -> 发现问题 -> 让 Agent 分析原因 -> 让 Agent 出解决方案 -> 确认方案♻️ -> 更新 Skill -> 自己用 ♻️
posts.summary: Skill 和软件一样，需要不断迭代的，而且你用户越多，遇到的各种问题就越多，就需要去解决各种边边角角的问题，才能越来越好用。

codexAnalysis.llmSummary: 宝玉总结 Skill 迭代方法：自己用、发现问题、让 Agent 复现分析并修复，再把经验沉淀进 Skill 和测试覆盖。
codexAnalysis.insight: 这是一套非常具体的 Skill/agent 产品迭代闭环，把使用现场转化为可复用能力。
codexAnalysis.whyItMatters: 直接适用于当前 X 雷达 skill 和前端体验的持续改进。
codexAnalysis.action: 把“复现问题 -> agent 分析 -> 更新 skill -> 加测试”作为本项目后续迭代流程。
codexAnalysis.risk: 案例细节依赖截图，离线文本看不到修复前后具体效果。

posts.name: Zephyr
posts.text:
It's not impossible to match Mythos
All u need is a solid 3T-4T parameter base model and super solid post-training (which Zhipu has the capability for)
Mythos Preview didn't receive proper post-training in Feb
This also means that Anthropic will have a super-strong model by Q4 due to better post-training of the Mythos base model

posts.name: 车厘子
posts.text:
前段时间查了一下，OpenAlice 有 38% 的流量都是从 Windows 来的。

我过去还以为一个纯开发者社区出来的工具流量大头至少是 Mac，实际上连开发者工具阶段（没打包）都有 38% 的 Windows，更别提后续产品化了。

怎么说呢，做运营做转化确实是个小马过河的事情，自己做了才有体会啊。
posts.summary: 前段时间查了一下，OpenAlice 有 38% 的流量都是从 Windows 来的。

posts.name: Serenity
posts.text:
Wow, I completely missed this with $LPK meeting notes. 

And I think markets did too. My biggest takeaway:

- NASDAQ listing is actively on their radar and are in discussions.
- 70% market share targeted, unholy target. 
- TAM greatly exceeds former projections.
- See a 4-5 base case of players this year with orders. 
- Industry is ready for high volume ramps. 

I do think LPK is very undervalued based on these discussions (disclosure: own positions).

posts.name: Serenity
posts.text:
I think something to highlight also is not all my ideas are green, especially on short term timeframes!

My core three themes are Neoclouds (Energy), Memory, and Photonics. 

And I'm glad I chose the literal top performers for each segment from $NBIS to $EWY leaps to $SIVE. 

However, I still have pretty large losses following the false analyst report on CPO delays that $NVDA refuted:

That nuked 3 of my TW CPO longs from Foci, Msscorp, Xintec and others. (which are heavily red). Shunsin / Win Semi are holding up much better though. 

Some of the other ones in Japan that I've mentioned like Towa, Harmonic Drive, NCI, etc. are performing much better following short-term volatility. 

As for Korea yes I have PTSD for from Auros and now Foosung that are both both red (I didn't own 093370 though, just got PTSD watching price action). 

Idk if I'll touch Korea again, just way too volatile. But I still think those will end up green eventually following Sk hynix/samsung qualifications + HVM, and the future japanese supply chain shutdown. 

I also did change some of my previous long ideas like $XLU following the Iran War nuking all chances of rate cuts from 3-4 down to 0, and that didn't play out too well. Three software names I mentioned previously like $TTD, $META ended up red. $SNAP, I also changed my thesis after noticing the endless SBC accounting methods. $RDDT idea was finally in the green from $130 -> $170 after a long time. 

But I think the vast majority of my ideas like $MRVL, $NBIS, $ARM, $INTC, $MU, $LITE, SK Hynix, Samsung, for larger cap. 

Down to $AXTI, $LITE, $AAOI, $RPI, $IQE, and others for smaller cap positions directionally play out pretty well. 

With random stuff like $SIMO, $HPS.A, $TSEM, $AEHR, $LPK, $SOI, $ALRIB, and so on all ended up turning out aight too. 

The blended average is kinda overwhelmingly green since majority of my long ideas are triple digit YTD.  

But I've definitely missed a few. 

Also entry point is really important too... I mentioned $AAOI at $30 or $AXTI at ~$13, but not everyone has the same entry point. So if someone bought AOI at $220 and it dropped to $160, I'd feel bad. 

But regardless, I'd prefer to judge how ideas play out on medium term timeframes over a few months rather than a few weeks.
posts.summary: I think something to highlight also is not all my ideas are green, especially on short term timeframes!

posts.name: Musolsol
posts.text:
倘若你拥有一种超能力，能让你倾心之人也对你钟情，你会选择使用吗？

若你心中正深爱着一个人，你能否凭借自由意志，让自己停止爱他？

你的理性或许会告诉你，他并非合适的恋爱对象，你甚至可以选择离开他、与他分手。

但很遗憾，自由意志无法抹杀你对他的爱意。

这是华语辩坛老友赛中，一段颠覆爱情观的结辩，想跟大家分享一下。

当反方坚持认为使用这种超能力是不尊重、不道德、不理性的行为时，正方四辩王景峰直接釜底抽薪，提出：
如果不爱一个人，你想让自己爱上他，几乎是不可能的。
你的理性或许会告诉你他很好，是个不错的恋爱对象，但抱歉，你无法因此产生爱的感觉。

四个论点一穿七，这段辩论至今仍被誉为“忆亡妻佳作”。

首先，他重新定义爱：“我有一个标准ABCD，满足的我爱，不满足的我就不爱。比如他很丑，我就不爱。” 
他指出，对方的误解就在于此，标准是留给不爱的人的。当真正爱的人出现，即便他一条标准都不符合，你依然会不可救药地沉沦。

“爱是自由意志的沉沦！”我们总以为爱是选择，是理性，是可以控制的喜欢。
但真正的爱从来不是“我选择喜欢你”，而是不想心动却照样心动，想放下却思念不受控制。爱不是自由选择，是你明知不该沉沦，却还是心甘情愿地沉下去，这才是爱的本质。

在定义完爱之后，他完成了关键的逻辑切割：“爱上和爱下去是两回事。”

 对方可能认为，他爱上我，我就可以予取予求，甚至虐待、殴打、折磨他，他也会始终爱我、不离开我。但这是错误的，爱上和爱下去是两回事。他有理性，他可以选择离开你，在是否要和你生活这件事上，他拥有自由意志。

“爱上去没什么了不起，爱下去才了不起！”

超能力只是让他瞬间爱上你，但能否一直爱下去，靠的是往后余生的经营和陪伴。

使用超能力剥夺了对方爱上别人的可能性，所以当对方爱上你的瞬间，爱的底色就变成了亏欠。而正是因为这种亏欠，我们才更可能珍惜这份爱、经营这份爱。选择使用这份超能力，不是为了永久控制对方，而是敢于承诺用一辈子去配得上这份突如其来的爱，将自私的控制扭转为负责任的开始。

紧接着，他戳中所有暗恋者的软肋：“我要把超能力还给月老，由月老去决定我爱的人会爱上谁。可他会爱上谁，那个人对他好还是坏，爱他还是不爱他，你都不知道。你这么爱他，怎么放心把他交给一个未知的某某某？”

反方提出“我尊重他的选择”，但在王景峰看来，尊重等于放任。“你口口声声说爱，却连‘我能对他更好’的自信和担当都没有。真正的爱是想要亲自给她幸福，而不是把她交给未知。” 

一句话，把克制说成了不够爱。最后，他撕开所有伪装，说：“你说你爱的理性、克制，我说你爱的好懦弱。” 这句话一出，屏幕前的人无不疯狂：“用！我现在就要用！”

当反方认为不使用是因为尊重、有底线时，王景峰反问：“你不敢用，真的是因为高尚吗？还是不敢面对失败，不敢承担责任？” 太多人用理性、体面、边界感包装自己，实则躲在理性克制的壳里自我感动。

回头看这场辩论，最厉害的并非讨论是否使用超能力，而是逼问我们每个人：
你到底是在尊重爱情，还是不敢去爱？
posts.summary: 倘若你拥有一种超能力，能让你倾心之人也对你钟情，你会选择使用吗？
